what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment record for the design-assets channel; it declares an image attachment with a unique id, a target URI, and an added timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
standouts: References an image asset via a URI-like target containing spaces and parentheses
Minimal metadata with no title, description, or dimensions
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with a stable att- prefixed id
Channel-memory attachment schema with channel, attachment_type, and target fields
concerns: Target string contains spaces and parentheses, which may be hard to resolve or link consistently
No MIME type, file size, checksum, or author information
No human-readable title or description for the asset
notes: A bare attachment ledger entry that points to a design-assets image but carries almost no descriptive context.
questions: What is the actual file or resource at image://design-assets/icons/image (19)1?
Who added this attachment and why?
What format, dimensions, or usage context does the image have?
Is the target URI resolvable by the consuming system?
purpose_vs_actual: The file's purpose is to record an image attachment in a channel, and it does exactly that with minimal metadata.
intention_signals: It appears to be registering a specific image asset in the design-assets channel so it can be referenced or discovered later.
novelty: The target URI scheme image:// and a path with spaces/parentheses is slightly unusual; otherwise the record is a conventional attachment ledger entry.
contribution: Provides a dated, typed pointer to a design asset within a specific channel, giving the fused system a concrete attachment reference to preserve.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata record att-b846e5d5 in the design-assets channel pointing to image://design-assets/icons/image (19)1, added 2026-06-22.
intention_for_embedding: To catalog an image asset attachment under the design-assets channel for reference or retrieval.
